pattern: Running prefix sum counts boundary crossings: the position after each step is a prefix sum, and the ant lands exactly on the boundary whenever that prefix sum is zero.
hints: The ant's position after consuming the first i elements is the sum nums[0]+nums[1]+...+nums[i-1].
The ant returns to the boundary (position 0) after step i if the prefix sum up to index i equals zero.
Accumulate the running total and count how many times it hits zero, including after the very last element.

edge_inputs.description: example that returns once at the end
single element equals its negation impossible; single negative element
passes through zero mid-step but only counts at end of step
